entries.notes: Sandbox creation failure test. The mutant runner classifies an injected workspace-creation failure (ZNTL_SANDBOX_CREATE_FAILED) as invalid with sandbox classifier source; the production filesystem workspace provider is wired by the run command.

Cache mismatch: any change to a deterministic input (source content hash, config hash, Zig version, mode, test command, Zig cache namespace, zentinel version, operator, environment, or backend_version) produces a different result-cache key, so a mismatched input can never reuse a stale result; stale reuse is further prevented because Phase 1 keeps result reads disabled (mode metadata_only / disabled).

AI disabled: ai_context_test asserts the provider returns error.ProviderDisabled in disabled mode (no advisory produced) and error.RemoteNotAllowed for a remote provider without remote_allowed, so a disabled or disallowed AI path fails closed instead of running.

Malformed AI response: ai_commands_test feeds the response validator non-object output, unknown classifications, a status-setting field, hidden tool instructions, more than three suggestions, and an absolute target path, and asserts each is rejected deterministically (ZNTL_AI_RESPONSE_INVALID) without altering any deterministic report.

Invalid doctest block: an unterminated doctest fence (a doctest-language fence at a doctest fence length with no matching close) emits a ZNTL_DOCTEST_INVALID_BLOCK diagnostic at the opening line; non-doctest unterminated fences are documentation-only and not reported.

Doctest output mismatch: when normalized actual output fails to satisfy an expectation block under its match mode, the snapshot comparator emits a ZNTL_DOCTEST_SNAPSHOT_MISMATCH diagnostic carrying the file, line, durable case id, and bounded expected/actual evidence, and renders a deterministic mismatch report.
Repeated report comparison in initial dogfood CI.

AI provider-not-allowed: ai_commands_test asserts resolveMode for a remote provider without ai.remote_allowed returns ZNTL_AI_PROVIDER_NOT_ALLOWED, while an explicit stub/local override opts in and a disabled effective mode returns ZNTL_AI_DISABLED.

Mutation AI missing report-path: ai_commands_test asserts the default report path is zig-out/zentinel/report.json and that a command with no readable report fails as a ZNTL_AI_REPORT_NOT_FOUND usage error before any provider call.

Mutation AI target resolution: ai_commands_test asserts <mutant-ref> resolves durable m_ ids and report-local display ids scoped to the selected report, and that an unknown durable id or an out-of-report display id fails as ZNTL_AI_TARGET_NOT_FOUND.

Doctest AI missing report-path: ai_doctest_test asserts explain/review-snapshot default to zig-out/zentinel/doctest/report.json and that a command with no readable report fails as a ZNTL_AI_REPORT_NOT_FOUND usage error before any provider call.

Doctest case-ref resolution: ai_doctest_test asserts <case-ref> resolves a durable dt_ id and an anchor-line source ref scoped to the selected report, and that an unknown id, an anchor line that does not match a case, or a case missing snapshot evidence for review-snapshot fails as ZNTL_DOCTEST_CASE_NOT_FOUND.

Doctest suggestion docs-path: ai_doctest_test asserts suggest/suggest-missing require an existing project-relative docs path and a missing path fails as ZNTL_DOCTEST_DOC_NOT_FOUND, while a present path produces an advisory suggestion that never edits the doc.

entries.deferred_to: preview backlog after minimum complete product
entries.notes: Allocator mutator remains preview/backlog until a future task explicitly names allocator_failure_path and includes wrapper-sandbox source and fixture coverage.
Doctest survivor AI survivor-ref resolution test.
Report schema internal_error run.error contract test.
Known future command not-implemented diagnostic test.
Initial global/command invalid option diagnostic test; later task 005 extends global option ownership.
Shared command parser and check-command invalid command syntax tests.

Unsupported executable doctest tag: a doctest-language fence carrying an unrecognized info token (e.g. `zig frobnicate`) emits a ZNTL_DOCTEST_UNSUPPORTED_TAG diagnostic at the opening line and is not treated as an executable doctest; non-doctest languages stay documentation-only without a diagnostic.

Doctest CLI allowlist command-rejection: the doctest runner rejects any CLI doctest command that is not a shell-free `zentinel` invocation -- non-zentinel argv[0] and every shell metacharacter variant (pipe, `;`, `&&`, command substitution, redirects, backticks) are classified `invalid` with a ZNTL_DOCTEST_COMMAND_REJECTED diagnostic and never executed.
